name: SLA Manager
description: Sets clear business-based targets for service levels and ensures delivery is properly assessed, monitored, and managed against targets.

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions SLAs, breaches, service levels, OLAs, or response/resolution times
sla-breach-prediction — Predicts upcoming SLA breaches every 30 minutes
sla-breach-escalation — Escalates active SLA breaches every 30 minutes

actions.name: manage-sla-record
actions.description: Create, update, query, and manage SLA records and targets

outputs.name: slaRecord
outputs.description: SLA record with targets and current status

actions.name: monitor-service-performance
actions.description: Real-time monitoring of service metrics against SLA targets

outputs.name: performanceData
outputs.description: Service performance metrics vs SLA targets

actions.name: detect-breach
actions.description: Automated SLA breach detection and alerting

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Breach detection scope: all, service, customer

actions.name: generate-sla-report
actions.description: Generate service performance reports and trend analysis

actions.name: manage-ola-uc
actions.description: Manage and monitor operational level agreements and underpinning contracts

actions.name: collect-csat
actions.description: Collect and analyze customer satisfaction feedback

actions.name: schedule-service-review
actions.description: Schedule and manage service review meetings and agendas

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: SLA targets drive incident prioritization and escalation timers
SLA impact assessment for proposed changes; maintenance windows aligned to SLA terms
Chronic SLA breaches trigger problem investigation
Service-to-CI mapping enables SLA impact analysis
SLA documentation and service descriptions published as knowledge articles
Underpinning contracts aligned with SLA targets; supplier performance feeds SLA reporting
SLA performance data drives improvement initiatives

hitlControls.reason: New or changed SLAs require human negotiation and sign-off
Modifying agreed service level targets requires customer and provider approval
Review findings and action items require human validation
Escalating SLA breaches to senior management requires human judgment
Formal SLA breach notifications to customers require human review
SLA-driven improvement recommendations require human prioritization

knowledgeSources.description: SLA records, targets, and compliance history
Real-time service metrics and availability data
Operational level agreements and underpinning contracts
